heading: Filing of habeas corpus application; time requirements; tolling rules
label: 2263 Filing of habeas corpus application; time requirements; tolling rules
notes: Effective DateSection applicable to cases pending on or after , see , set out as a note under .Apr. 24, 1996section 107(c) of Pub. L. 104–132section 2261 of this title

children.label: a
children.text: Any application under this chapter for habeas corpus relief under section 2254 must be filed in the appropriate district court not later than 180 days after final State court affirmance of the conviction and sentence on direct review or the expiration of the time for seeking such review.

from the date that a petition for certiorari is filed in the Supreme Court until the date of final disposition of the petition if a State prisoner files the petition to secure review by the Supreme Court of the affirmance of a capital sentence on direct review by the court of last resort of the State or other final State court decision on direct review;
from the date on which the first petition for post-conviction review or other collateral relief is filed until the final State court disposition of such petition; and

children.label: A
children.text: a motion for an extension of time is filed in the Federal district court that would have jurisdiction over the case upon the filing of a habeas corpus application under section 2254; and

children.label: B
children.text: a showing of good cause is made for the failure to file the habeas corpus application within the time period established by this section.
